Package de.bsommerfeld.pathetic.engine.pathfinder.heap

Class QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap

java.lang.Object
de.bsommerfeld.pathetic.engine.pathfinder.heap.QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap
public class QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap extends Object
A quaternary (4-ary) min-heap implementation optimized for pathfinding algorithms.

This heap uses primitive arrays for memory efficiency and cache locality, making it particularly suitable for performance-critical pathfinding operations. Each node in the heap can have up to 4 children, which reduces the height of the tree compared to a binary heap, resulting in fewer comparisons during sift operations.

The heap maintains two parallel arrays and a HashMap:

  • heap: stores packed node coordinates (long) in heap order
  • costs: stores the corresponding costs for each node
  • nodeToIndexMap: provides O(1) lookup from packed coordinate to heap position

This implementation supports efficient decrease-key operations through the insertOrUpdate(long, double) method, which is essential for pathfinding algorithms like A* and Dijkstra's algorithm. The heap automatically resizes when capacity is exceeded.

Since:
5.4.2

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap()
    Constructs a new quaternary min-heap with the default initial capacity.

    Constructs a new quaternary min-heap with the specified initial capacity.

  • Constructor Details

    • QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap

      public QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap()
      Constructs a new quaternary min-heap with the default initial capacity.
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap

      public QuaternaryPrimitiveMinHeap(int initialCapacity)
      Constructs a new quaternary min-heap with the specified initial capacity.
      Parameters:
      initialCapacity - the initial size of the heap arrays
      Since:
      5.4.2

  • Method Details

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns whether the heap is empty.
      Returns:
      true if the heap contains no elements
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of elements in the heap.
      Returns:
      the size of the heap
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Removes all elements from the heap.
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • contains

      public boolean contains(long packedNode)
      Checks if a specific node (packed coordinate) is currently in the heap.
      Parameters:
      packedNode - the packed coordinate to check
      Returns:
      true if the node is in the heap
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • getCost

      public double getCost(long packedNode)
      Gets the current cost of a node in the heap.
      Parameters:
      packedNode - the packed coordinate
      Returns:
      the cost, or Double.MAX_VALUE if not present
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • insertOrUpdate

      public void insertOrUpdate(long packedNode, double cost)
      Inserts a new node or updates an existing node's cost in the heap.

      If the node is not currently in the heap, it is inserted with the given cost. If the node already exists and the new cost is lower, the node's cost is updated (decrease-key operation). If the new cost is higher or equal, no operation is performed.

      Parameters:
      packedNode - the packed coordinate of the node
      cost - the cost associated with the node
      Since:
      5.4.2

    • extractMin

      public long extractMin()
      Removes and returns the node with the minimum cost from the heap.

      This operation takes O(log n) time due to the sift-down operation needed to restore the heap property after removing the root element.

      Returns:
      the packed coordinate of the node with the minimum cost
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if the heap is empty
      Since:
      5.4.2
